name: architecture
description: Timeplus/Proton architecture and codebase layout covering directories, modules, two-layer storage, streaming core, module dependency hierarchy, and IProcessor pipeline. Use when user asks to understand design decisions, locate code, or trace component interactions and data flow.
---

# Architecture

## Core design

- C++20 streaming SQL engine extending ClickHouse with streaming semantics
- Streaming by default (`SELECT FROM stream`), historical via `table(stream)`
- All streams have implicit `_tp_time datetime64(3, 'UTC')` for event-time tracking
- Materialized Views = continuous queries with checkpointing
- Two-layer storage per shard: WAL (streaming store) + historical store

## Two-layer storage model

```
Ingestion → [ Streaming Store (WAL/NativeLog) ] → async compaction → [ Historical Store (Columnar) ]
                 ↓ incremental processing                              ↓ analytical/backfill queries
              streaming queries                                     table() historical queries
```

| Layer | Purpose | Backed by |
|-------|---------|-----------|
| Streaming Store | Ingestion, incremental processing, replication | NativeLog (WAL) |
| Historical Store | Compressed columnar data for analytics/backfill | MergeTree-based engine |

Storage types: `hybrid` (both, default), `streaming` (WAL only), `inmemory` (WAL in memory, single-instance)

## Source directory quick lookup

| Path | Contains |
|------|----------|
| `src/Parsers/` | SQL parsing |
| `src/Interpreters/` | Query execution logic |
| `src/Interpreters/Streaming/` | Streaming-specific interpreters |
| `src/Storages/Stream/` | StorageStream, streaming sources/sinks (Proton-specific) |
| `src/Storages/MatView/` | Materialized View implementation |
| `src/Functions/` | SQL functions |
| `src/AggregateFunctions/` | Aggregation functions |
| `src/Processors/` | Query pipeline processors |
| `src/Processors/Transforms/Streaming/` | 99+ streaming transforms |
| `src/Processors/QueryPlan/Streaming/` | 42 streaming query plan steps |
| `src/Cluster/` | Clustering (strict dependency hierarchy) |
| `programs/server/` | proton server |
| `programs/client/` | proton client |
| `tests/queries_ported/` | Stateless SQL tests (`.sql` + `.reference`) |

Full layout → [references/source-layout.md](references/source-layout.md)

## Module dependency hierarchy (top → bottom)

```
MetaStore, Replica → NativeLog, KafkaLog → Raft → Requests → Protocol → Common
```

- [ ] NEVER introduce circular dependencies
- [ ] Respect component boundaries
- [ ] See `src/Cluster/README.md` for details

Full diagram → [references/cluster-deps.md](references/cluster-deps.md)

## IProcessor pipeline pattern

All streaming components follow:
```cpp
class IProcessor {
    virtual Status prepare();  // O(1), non-blocking readiness check
    virtual void work();       // Actual processing
    enum Status { NeedData, PortFull, Ready, Finished, Async };
};
```

Stateful processors MUST implement: `hasState()`, `checkpoint()`, `recover()`

## Reserved fields

| Field | Type | Purpose |
|-------|------|---------|
| `_tp_time` | `datetime64(3, 'UTC')` | Event timestamp (auto-added to all streams) |
| `_tp_delta` | `int8` | Change type: 1=insert, -1=delete (changelog only) |
| `window_start` | `datetime64` | Window boundary (auto-generated by window functions) |
| `window_end` | `datetime64` | Window boundary (auto-generated by window functions) |

## Watermarks

- Advance monotonically per stream based on event-time progress (not wall-clock)
- Determine when time windows can be safely closed
- Three streaming query trigger types:

| Query type | Trigger |
|-----------|---------|
| Non-aggregation | When events arrive |
| Window aggregation | Window end + watermark |
| Global aggregation | Fixed interval (default 2s) |
